---
title: "Help me prune my xorg.conf - Ubuntu 8.04.2"
date: 2009-05-10
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by crjackson on 2009-05-10
I just recovered from a major screw up. I tried to install an updated video driver using envy and nearly had to re-install ubuntu on the wife's laptop. Major mistake.

Anyway I got it working but now my xorg.conf has a bunch of junk left behind from failed attempts at fixing.

I want to cut out the fat, but I'm not going to do this again without some help.

Here's the contents:

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@vernadsky)  Thu Jun  5 09:26:53 UTC 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Configured Monitor"
    VendorName     "Generic LCD Display"
    ModelName      "LCD Panel 1280x800"
    HorizSync       31.5 - 50.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 65.0
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x768@60" 80.1 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x720@60" 74.5 1280 1336 1472 1664 720 721 724 746 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x800@60" 83.5 1280 1344 1480 1680 800 801 804 828 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
 #   
    Identifier     "monitor1"
    Gamma           1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "AUO"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 75.0
    VertRefresh     60.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Configured Video Device"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BoardName      "vesa"
    Option         "NoLogo" "True"
    BusID          "PCI:0:18:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
 #   
    Identifier     "device1"
    Driver         "vesa"
    BoardName      "vesa"
    BusID          "PCI:0:18:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7150M / nForce 630M"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Configured Video Device"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Virtual     1280 800
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x800@60" "1280x720@60" "1280x768@60" "800x600@60" "800x600@56"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
 #   
    Identifier     "screen1"
    Device         "device1"
    Monitor        "monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x800 +0+0; 800x600@60 +0+0"
EndSection

```

This is a single monitor sytem, I have no idea why it has TwinView stuff. I want to remove the extra entries and get rid of the nvidia splash screen.

Thanks for any help.

### Post by crjackson on 2009-05-11
bump

### Post by Paddy Landau on 2009-05-11
I'm not an expert on this. However, I can offer you this:

[LIST]
[*]**Make a backup of xorg.conf first!** If you don't, then you can't restore.
[/LIST]
Reset your xorg.conf to the defaults with the following command.
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```You should be able to slowly build the xorg.conf from there.

HTH

---

Reset your xorg.conf to the defaults with the following command.
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```You should be able to slowly build the xorg.conf from there.

HTH

I'll probably end up doing that. I was kind of hoping someone could take a look at it and see some obvious things that need to be cut out of the file.

Thanks for your response.

### Post by philinux on 2009-05-11
The new xorg.conf is meant to be very sparse in fact it is destined for oblivian in the fullness of time. You could use xfix from recovery too.

---

### Post by unutbu on 2009-05-11
I don't know if this will work, but I've tried pruning out some stanzas that were not being used:

First, backup your current xorg.conf:
```

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf{,-20090511}
```

Modified /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
```

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@vernadsky)  Thu Jun  5 09:26:53 UTC 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "AUO"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 75.0
    VertRefresh     60.0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7150M / nForce 630M"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
#     Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x800 +0+0; 800x600@60 +0+0"
EndSection

```

### Post by Didius Falco on 2009-05-11
Have you tried looking in the directory to see how many backups there are, and the last time/date stamp? It could be that there is a backup prior to your misadventure yesterday.

BTW, I'm glad you survived that episode. I shudder to think what my wife would do to me if I screwed up her PC...

Regards,

Didius
